content:
85,000,000 people died during World War II (a reminder/for perspective: 3,000 in 9/11; 200,000 during the chaos of 2020 [so far]). Under the failed painter and speed addict, Germany was responsible for about 12 million of those deaths; 6 million Jews, 7 million Soviet civilians, 1.8 million Polish civilians, and the rest of that number is made up of the handicapped, Roma (Gypsies), homosexuals, and other non-German scapegoats/minorities -- all this occurred under the guise of Us vs Them.

Unfortunately, it is Us vs Them; but we don't realize we are the Them because the lines are being obfuscated. It's not Left vs Right, it's the 1% vs the 99%, the rich vs the poor, the gluttonous bourgeoise vs the working people of this country. We are all on the same team, but we're made to believe that we are enemies. We live in a time that is the least violent in all of recorded history -- yet, we are the most anxious and depressed and divided generation. Why?

In a world that spreads its legs for nostalgia and a penchant for franchises, it is up to us to insure their is no sequel to World War II or the American Civil War despite the Box Office predictions.
context: From document: On the yearly anniversary of 9/11 Americans all over the country share… — chunk 3 (chunk 3/4)
